originalTitle: JPMorgan Chase unveils $50 billion buyback, Goldman Sachs raises dividend after Fed stress test
summary: The announcements followed the release of the Federal Reserve's annual stress test, which found that all 32 large banks weathered a hypothetical recession.
content:
JPMorgan Chase on Wednesday unveiled a new $50 billion share repurchase program and raised its quarterly dividend after the Federal Reserve found the industry remained well capitalized under its annual stress test.

The biggest U.S. bank by assets said it will increase its quarterly dividend 10% to $1.65 per share, subject to board approval, and authorized the buyback program effective July 1.

"The Board's intended dividend increase is supported by our consistent investment in our business and strong financial performance," JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon said in a statement. "As always, we are prepared for a wide range of scenarios, including the hypothetical 2026 supervisory severely adverse scenario."

Goldman Sachs likewise increased its quarterly payouts, saying that its dividend will rise 11% to $5 per share, citing the firm's strong earnings and capital position.

Wells Fargo said it expects to raise its dividend by 11% to 50 cents per share, while Morgan Stanley boosted its payout 15% to $1.15 per share, while also reauthorizing a $20 billion buyback program.

Bank of America CEO Brian Moynihan said in a statement that the bank will make an announcement on the firm's dividend next month.

The announcements followed the release of the Federal Reserve's annual stress test , which found that all 32 large banks remained above their minimum capital requirements even after a hypothetical recession generating more than $708 billion in projected losses across the industry.

Unlike in previous years, however, the results will not affect banks' capital requirements. The Fed said earlier this year it would keep stress capital buffers unchanged through 2027 while it overhauls the testing methodology, meaning banks entered Wednesday with a clear understanding of their capital requirements.

While analysts had expected the exercise to have little immediate impact, in a sign of confidence, banks opted to proceed with payout increases, despite the regulatory limbo.

In a note ahead of the results, KBW described this year's stress test as "going through the motions," arguing that investors are more focused on the pending Basel III Endgame proposal expected later this year than on the Fed's annual exercise.

This story is developing. Please check back for updates.
chineseTitle: 摩根大通宣布500亿美元回购 高盛在美联储压力测试后提高股息
chineseSummary: 这些公告是在美联储发布年度压力测试之后发布的，该测试发现所有 32 家大型银行都经受住了假设的衰退。
chineseContent:
在美联储发现该行业在年度压力测试中资本充足后，摩根大通周三公布了一项新的 500 亿美元股票回购计划，并提高了季度股息。

这家美国资产规模最大的银行表示，将在董事会批准的情况下将季度股息提高 10%，至每股 1.65 美元，并授权回购计划于 7 月 1 日生效。

摩根大通首席执行官杰米戴蒙在一份声明中表示：“董事会预期的股息增加得到了我们对业务的持续投资和强劲的财务业绩的支持。” “一如既往，我们为各种情况做好了准备，包括假设的 2026 年监管严重不利的情况。”

高盛同样提高了季度派息，称其股息将上涨 11% 至每股 5 美元，理由是该公司强劲的盈利和资本状况。

富国银行表示，预计将股息提高 11%，至每股 50 美分，而摩根士丹利则将派息提高 15%，至每股 1.15 美元，同时还重新授权一项 200 亿美元的回购计划。

美国银行首席执行官布莱恩·莫伊尼汉在一份声明中表示，该银行将于下个月宣布公司股息。

这些公告是在美联储发布年度压力测试之后发布的，该测试发现，即使在假设的经济衰退导致整个行业预计损失超过 7080 亿美元之后，所有 32 家大型银行仍然高于最低资本要求。

然而，与前几年不同的是，这一结果不会影响银行的资本要求。美联储今年早些时候表示，将在 2027 年之前保持压力资本缓冲不变，同时彻底改革测试方法，这意味着银行在周三就对其资本要求有了清晰的了解。

尽管分析师预计此举不会产生什么直接影响，但出于信心的迹象，银行选择继续增加派息，尽管存在监管困境。

在结果公布前的一份报告中，KBW 将今年的压力测试描述为“走过场”，认为投资者更关注预计今年晚些时候悬而未决的巴塞尔协议 III 终局提案，而不是美联储的年度演习。

这个故事正在发展。请回来查看更新。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
